title: Overgrown's Sakura Sword
description: Add a custom CIT 3D sword that you obtain by renaming any sword or axe item
body:
**IF YOU ARE USING VERSIONS BELOW 1.21 (Otherwise, ignore)**: Requires the mod [OptiFine](https://optifine.net/home) or [CIT Resewn](https://modrinth.com/mod/cit-resewn) if you are using [Fabric](https://fabricmc.net/).

**This Pack Adds**:
- Custom 3D Weapon

**Texture Pack Detail**:

- **Sakura Sword** (Rename _any_ vanilla **Axe** or **Sword** item)

I wanted to create more models and was lacking inspiration. So, I saw my friend, Mrs. [Giovanka](https://solo.to/giovanka) of the Giovanka brand was wielding a sword called "Sakura Sword". I liked the idea of a Sakura themed sword and asked her if I could make a custom weapon from it and how would she wanted to look. She said a sword with a white blade, mimicking the traditional [japanese katana](https://www.google.com/search?q=japanese+katana&source=lnms&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwiw3_L1h6T9AhW3lIkEHZlsC00Q0pQJegQIBRAC&biw=1707&bih=841&dpr=1.5&udm=2). I then decided that to keep the Sakura theme, I would make the grip a pink color and add some [Sakura Flowers](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cherry_blossom) to it, this way it keeps the Sakura theme while being stylistically consistent with theme and tone.

I think it turned out quite well. One thing I might change in the future is the [Crossguard](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Crossguard), since katanas have a circular crossguard called [Tsuba](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Japanese_sword_mountings#Tsuba). Since I don't know how to make circles in [Blockbench](https://www.blockbench.net/) yet, it might be a while before I figure that out
